bd.title: Proposed National Budget Faces Scrutiny from Economists and Businesses
bd.summary: The recently unveiled Tk 7.97 trillion national budget for FY2024-25 is drawing mixed reactions from economists and business leaders. Concerns are being raised regarding its ambitious revenue targets, inflation control measures, and potential impact on various sectors. Discussions are ongoing in parliament ahead of its final approval, with experts urging realistic adjustments.

bd.title: BNP Demands Fair Trial for Party Leaders, Alleges Political Persecution
bd.summary: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) has reiterated its demand for a fair judicial process for its arrested leaders and activists, alleging political motivation behind the charges. Party spokespersons claim the government is suppressing dissent through politically motivated cases, hindering democratic space. This comes amidst ongoing political tensions between the ruling party and the opposition ahead of general elections.

bd.title: International Aid Agencies Urge Renewed Focus on Rohingya Repatriation Efforts
bd.summary: Several international aid organizations are pressing for renewed diplomatic efforts and support for the safe and dignified repatriation of Rohingya refugees to Myanmar. With over a million refugees living in camps in Cox's Bazar, the prolonged crisis continues to pose significant humanitarian and logistical challenges for Bangladesh. The call comes as global attention often shifts to other crises, potentially impacting funding and long-term solutions.

international.title: Ukraine Intensifies Drone Attacks on Russian Territory Amidst Eastern Front Fighting
international.summary: Ukraine has escalated its drone attacks targeting various regions deep within Russia, including energy infrastructure, in recent days. Simultaneously, intense ground fighting continues along the eastern front, particularly near Pokrovsk and Chasiv Yar, as both sides seek tactical advantages. International observers are monitoring the effectiveness of new Western military aid on the battlefield as Kyiv aims to disrupt Russian logistics.

international.title: Gaza Ceasefire Talks Stalled as Israeli Forces Advance Deeper into Rafah
international.summary: Efforts to secure a ceasefire in Gaza appear stalled after Israeli forces continued their advance deeper into Rafah, despite international pressure and ongoing mediation attempts. Humanitarian organizations warn of a worsening crisis for civilians trapped in the area, facing severe shortages of food, water, and medical supplies. Diplomatic efforts involving the US, Egypt, and Qatar are struggling to bridge the gaps between the warring parties amidst conflicting demands.

international.title: US Presidential Candidates Ramp Up Campaigning Ahead of Key Debates
international.summary: The leading US presidential candidates are intensifying their campaign efforts across swing states as the election season heats up, with key debates approaching. Polls suggest a tight race, and both campaigns are focusing on economic policy, immigration, and foreign affairs to sway undecided voters. The outcome is being closely watched globally for its potential implications on international relations, trade, and global stability.
